subject: Default VES Collector port set to 302417
commit_message:
Default VES Collector port set to 302417

This patch includes an update to VES Event Listener specification, where a default VES Collector port is updated from 8443 to 30417.
This is a default ONAP/Frankfurt configuration, and this is how network function instances will be reaching VES collector.

Change-Id: I8696d706916c9e16251bd50744d49b6deebc4fa1
Signed-off-by: deen1985 <damian.nowak@nokia.com>
Issue-ID: VNFRQTS-887

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/1541/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/rtdv3-global-verify-master/1541

Patch Set 1:

Adding required reviewers per https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/VES+Specification+Reviewers+and+Process

If you do not wish to be tagged in VES reviews, please remove yourself from that wiki page.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

I'm OK pushing this into 7.1.1, but I do want to be clear on what that means to 3GPP.  Due to the timing of standard approval in 3GPP, we were pushed to provide a link to the specification prior to the end of the ONAP Frankfurt release.  I attempted to provide them a link to the document in Github using the Frankfurt branch, but both 3GPP and the ONAP TSC felt uncomfortable pointing at a branch that could technically change so they insisted on referring to a specific commit.

This means if we change this, the 3GGP spec will not see the change (at least without going through the whole notification process again and potentially impacting the relevant 3GPP documents as well).

This is primarily an informational change and does not materially change the specification.

I see 3 options:

1) We push it in, and leave 3GPP as-is
2) We defer this to 7.2
3) We push it in, and notify 3GPP of the change and try to bring them in sync

My personal vote is for #2, but I'm OK with #1.  I'm not in favor of #3, but if enough people are then I'll see what we can do.

Thanks,
Trevor

=[gh]=

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Well, actually, best would be to remove this sentence entirely.
Actually a lot depends on the Kubernetes platform configuration, where this Collector will be running.
It can use:
- NodePort, as today - and here the external port will not be 8443, as many containers expose 8443 internally.
- ClusterIP - the the container might get a dedicated IP addressable externaly, and port 8443 will be exposed.
- Yet another solution might be introduced with Istio Service Mesh.

Based on that, removing this sentence at all will be probably a good idea.
I am as well fine to defer thsi to VES 7.2, esp. after a relevant commit has been share with 3GPP (which I wasn`t really aware of).

Shall I abandon this MR, or do You see another solution here?
